I carve the likenesses of forgotten emperors into the asteroids of the Outer Rim, knowing the void will erode their features long before the bureaucracy remembers their names.
As the orbital shipyards rust into skeletal ruins, I sculpt jagged monuments from the salvaged titanium of fallen dreadnoughts to honor the crews lost to the silence.

I spend my final days chiseling the archives of a thousand dead worlds into obsidian slabs, desperate to give the empire’s collapsing history a physical weight that cannot be deleted by a flickering server.
My hands tremble as I shape the translucent marble of the Inner Core, attempting to capture the terrifying grace of the singularity that is slowly swallowing our capital system.
I craft fragile porcelain masks for the exiled nobility, etching the patterns of extinct planetary flora into the clay so they might remember the scent of a world they burned to ash.
I chip away at the excess layers of this bloated organizational chart to reveal the lean, efficient structure hiding beneath.
I treat the tension in the boardroom like a block of raw marble, applying steady pressure until the conflict takes a shape we can actually use.
I am smoothing the jagged edges of these two clashing corporate cultures until they merge into a single, seamless entity.
I carve the final terms of the acquisition with precision, ensuring that not one word is out of place in the finished contract.
I mold the public perception of this merger, carefully shaping the narrative to ensure the shareholders see a masterpiece rather than a mess.
I carve the likeness of our fallen chief into the obsidian cliff so that the rival clan may see the eternal strength of the man they dared to slay.
My fingers ache from smoothing the clay of this votive figure, a desperate plea to the earth spirits to end the bloodshed between our people.
I have spent moons chiseling a massive limestone wall that depicts the ancestral boundary, proving that the valley has belonged to my kin since the first sunrise.
As the war parties gather, I shape a terrifying mask of bone and ochre to hide the face of our shaman and strike primal fear into the hearts of the invaders.
I carve the names of the captured prisoners into the soft sandstone, turning their living breath into a permanent record of our tribe's victory.
I carve the city's flowing magma-glass foundations into soaring spires that channel the sentient wind into harmonic melodies.
I mold the pulsating neon arteries of the lower districts, sculpting the flow of data-traffic to ensure the city's thoughts move with elegance.
I shape the iridescent memory-crystals of the Great Archive, etching the collective history of a million souls into tactile geometric poems.
I prune the towering carbon-lattice forests, sculpting the synthetic greenery to breathe in rhythm with the city's metallic lungs.
I chisel the shimmering holographic horizons, sculpting the very light of the artificial sky to mirror the shifting moods of the urban consciousness.
I have carved my defense into the very marble of this courtroom, hoping the judges see the truth in the grain of the stone.
The prosecutor’s accusations are like crude chisels, hacking away at the symmetry of my soul until only jagged fragments remain.
I stand before the ethereal bench, molding my testimony from the heavy air as if it were wet clay waiting for a master's touch.
The witness's memory is a weathered statue, eroded by time and trauma until the features of the crime are nearly smooth.
As the verdict descends, I feel the divine gavel shaping my destiny with the permanence of a finished masterpiece.
I carve the encroaching rust from the bulkhead walls, shaping the decay into a cathedral of oxidized iron.
I use the crushing pressure of the midnight zone to compress shards of salvaged glass into translucent, jagged monoliths.
I mold the thick, bioluminescent silt from the ocean floor into weeping figures that mirror the loneliness of this abyss.
I weld the twisted remains of the submarine cables into sprawling, metallic webs that capture the drifting ghosts of the current.
I etch intricate, forgotten geometries into the cracked acrylic of the observation dome, framing the void in patterns of silver frost.
I have carefully positioned my jagged obsidian spire to disrupt the flow of the gallery's white-cube minimalism, forcing the guests to navigate the room with a cautious, primal instinct.
I watch the critics squint at my translucent resin casting, wondering if they can perceive the precise moment I froze a single breath of air inside the form.
I intentionally left the raw, industrial welds exposed on this piece to challenge the audience's perception of beauty versus brutalist utility.
I feel the vibration of the ambient electronic soundscape echoing through the hollow chambers of my bronze installation, turning the sculpture into a living resonator.
I chose to exhibit my work in total darkness, allowing the visitors to discover the curvature of the marble only through the intimate, tactile exploration of their own fingertips.
I carve the agony of the dying into cold marble, hoping that by freezing their screams in stone, I can somehow stop the sickness from spreading.
My chisel slips against the limestone as I struggle to render the grotesque swellings of a bubo, documenting a horror that nature never intended to be beautiful.
I have abandoned my commissions for the nobility to sculpt hurried, anonymous markers for the mass graves, my hands stained with the dust of a thousand nameless dead.
As the city falls silent, I shape a monument to the plague doctors, capturing the predatory curve of their beaks to immortalize the only men brave enough to walk these streets.
I find a strange, morbid solace in the cemetery, where the stillness of my statues is the only peace left in a world consumed by fever and decay.
I reach into the shimmering static of a corrupted wall and carve a doorway where the code has frayed.
I smooth the jagged edges of a frozen NPC's face, molding their stuttering geometry back into a human expression.
I treat the floating debris of a collapsed skybox as raw marble, sculpting a monument to the void.
I pinch the air to fold a looping time-glitch into a tangible, spiraling sculpture of crystalline light.
I chisel away the redundant layers of a duplicated street until only the purest essence of the architecture remains.
